extraction.one_line_summary: DEMAR Arnaud won sprint after echelon chaos caught Yates, Bilbao, Vivo, and Mica before they regrouped on directional change.
tactical_patterns: Decuninck-Quickstep and Jumbo-Visma initiated echelons early on flat windswept stage, forcing GC riders into immediate defensive mode.
Directional course change at 80km allowed scattered GC riders to regroup, neutralizing earlier echelon damage from northern winds.
Demar sprints only when leadout man Démare present, revealing Groupama-FDJ's dual tactical structure for stage hunting.

rider_signals.YATES Simon: negative: caught behind echelons and required significant time to rejoin, showing poor positioning awareness on windy flat stage
rider_signals.BILBAO Pello: negative: caught in echelons despite GC position, questionable wind awareness for Basque climber defending podium
rider_signals.FULSANG Jakob: negative: missed first echelon group despite Astana's classics experience, required prolonged chase effort before directional change
rider_signals.ALMEIDA Joao: positive: avoided all crashes and echelons while in pink jersey, exceptional positioning for 22-year-old in first Grand Tour lead
rider_signals.VIVIANI Elia: positive: won intermediate sprint points over field sprinters, first competitive showing after disappointing early stages
rider_signals.DEMAR Arnaud: positive: third win in seven stages with Démare present indicates sprint train functioning at exceptional level
